short_summary: Iran ceasefire may ease energy costs
rationale: The confirmed US-Iran ceasefire and Hormuz reopening is the most relevant new development for Portugal, with potential to ease energy prices that affect cost of living and tourism competitiveness.

top_events.title: US, Iran announce ceasefire agreement; Strait of Hormuz reopening
top_events.summary: A confirmed Hormuz reopening could lower oil and gas prices, indirectly reducing energy costs for Portuguese households and businesses. Portugal is sensitive to EU-wide energy price movements.

top_events.title: Tourist train overturns and injures 17 during Cártama tapas festival
top_events.summary: A tourist train accident in nearby Spain (Cártama, Andalusia) is geographically close and relevant to Iberian tourism safety awareness, though impact on Portuguese daily life is minimal.

top_events.title: Protesters clash with police ahead of G7 summit in Geneva
top_events.summary: G7 protests in Geneva have no direct impact on Portuguese daily life; minor background noise for EU political environment.
